rulings.Case Title: NATALIE BUFORD et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al

rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, February 16, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Intervenors HARTFORD FIRE INSURANCE COMPANY, WEST AMERICAN INSURANCE COMPANY, SAFECO INSURANCE COMPANY OF AMERICA, TRAVELERS INDEMNITY CO., ZURICH AMERICAN INSURANCE CO., SUCCESSOR IN INTEREST TO ZURICH INSURANCE COMPANY, US BRANCH MOTION¿s motion for court approval of settlement as being in good faith and for dismissal will be heard on March 9, 2011 at 9 per the amended notice of motion. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: RODRICK BRECKLER et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, February 16, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Selman Breitman¿s motion for leave to withdraw as counsel is granted. Hearing required unless an attorney with Selman Breitman contacts a responsible representative of B.E.E. Industrial Supply and informs that person of the tentative ruling and is told that B.E.E. Industrial Supply does not contest the tentative ruling. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH HOGAN VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc.¿S Motion For Summary Judgment Is Continued On The Court¿S Own Motion To February 9, 2011 At 9:30 A.M. In Department 220
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, February 16, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 5. Defendant Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant satisfied its burden of production and plaintiff did not present any admissible evidence creating a triable issue whether he was exposed to joint compound or any other asbestos-containing product attributable to defendant. Defendant¿s objections to plaintiff¿s declaration are sustained. Defendant¿s request for sanctions is denied. While plaintiff¿s declaration contravened the D¿Amico rule, there is no basis to find that the declaration was submitted in bad faith or for the purpose of delay. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: DONALD GAUGER et al VS. ALCATEL LUCENT et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, February 16, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. Defendant Rockwell Automation, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted and plaintiffs¿ request for a further continuance of the hearing is denied. Defendant satisfied its burden of production and plaintiffs did not present any evidence creating a triable issue that Mr. Gauger was exposed to any asbestos-containing product attributable to defendant. Plaintiffs¿ supplemental responses which were served after the date the motion was filed are not considered in determining whether defendant met its burden of production. Plaintiffs failed to show that further discovery or investigation concerning Western Electric Company jobsites, the Collins Radio Company or Rockwell Collins, Inc, or the taking of the depositions of personnel from United Airlines and Boeing Corporation may result in evidence to oppose this motion. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: DONALD GAUGER et al VS. ALCATEL LUCENT et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Declaration Of Stan Ho In Support Of Rockwell Automation, Inc.¿S Motion For Summary Judgment Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication; Points And Authorities ; Request For Judicial Notice; [Proposed] Order
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, February 16, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. Plaintiffs¿ motion to strike the declaration of Stan Ho in support of Rockwell Automation, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment or, in the alternative, summary adjudication is taken off calendar as moot. Even without considering the declaration of Mr. Ho, defendant is entitled to summary judgment in its favor. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: DAVID J. PALOMARES VS. AC AND S, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, February 16, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 4. Defendant Tingue, Brown & Co.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to the first and second issues (negligence and strict liability), taken off calendar as to the third issue (punitive damages) and granted as to the fourth issue (Talley Machinery Corporation). Liberally construed, the deposition testimony of Mr. Hickok and Mr. Tingue, defendant¿s GO 129 interrogatory responses and the excerpts of the catalog attached to Mr. Tingue¿s deposition transcript create a triable issue that Mr. Palomares was exposed to asbestos-containing brown pads for a flat iron that are attributable to defendant. Dr. Horn¿s declaration creates a triable issue whether Mr. Palomares¿ exposure to the brown pads was a substantial factor in causing or contributing to his mesothelioma. Plaintiff dismissed his claim for punitive damages. Defendant satisfied its burden of production and plaintiff did not present any evidence that Talley Machinery Corporation (as distinct from Tingue, Brown & Co.) is legally responsible for any of the brown pads mentioned in Mr. Hickok¿s deposition testimony. = (220/HEK)
